segments.text: Then the LORD said to Moses,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Go to Pharaoh, for I have hardened his heart and the hearts of his officials, that I may perform these miraculous signs of Mine among them,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: and that you may tell your children and grandchildren how severely I dealt with the Egyptians when I performed miraculous signs among them, so that all of you may know that I am the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: So Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and told him,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: This is what the LORD, the God of the Hebrews, says: ‘How long will you refuse to humble yourself before Me? Let My people go, so that they may worship Me.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But if you refuse to let My people go, I will bring locusts into your territory tomorrow.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They will cover the face of the land so that no one can see it. They will devour whatever is left after the hail and eat every tree that grows in your fields.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They will fill your houses and the houses of all your officials and every Egyptian—something neither your fathers nor your grandfathers have seen since the day they came into this land.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then Moses turned and left Pharaoh’s presence.
Pharaoh’s officials asked him,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: How long will this man be a snare to us? Let the people go, so that they may worship the LORD their God. Do you not yet realize that Egypt is in ruins?
segments.speaker: Pharaoh’s officials

segments.text: So Moses and Aaron were brought back to Pharaoh.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Go, worship the LORD your God,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he said.
But who exactly will be going?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: We will go with our young and old,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Moses replied.
We will go with our sons and daughters, and with our flocks and herds, for we must hold a feast to the LORD.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Then Pharaoh told them,
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: May the LORD be with you if I ever let you go with your little ones. Clearly you are bent on evil.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: No, only the men may go and worship the LORD, since that is what you have been requesting.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: And Moses and Aaron were driven from Pharaoh’s presence.
Then the LORD said to Moses,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Stretch out your hand over the land of Egypt, so that the locusts may swarm over it and devour every plant in the land—everything that the hail has left behind.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So Moses stretched out his staff over the land of Egypt, and throughout that day and night the LORD sent an east wind across the land. By morning the east wind had brought the locusts.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: The locusts swarmed across the land and settled over the entire territory of Egypt. Never before had there been so many locusts, and never again will there be.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: They covered the face of all the land until it was black, and they consumed all the plants on the ground and all the fruit on the trees that the hail had left behind. Nothing green was left on any tree or plant in all the land of Egypt.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Pharaoh quickly summoned Moses and Aaron and said,
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: I have sinned against the LORD your God and against you.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Now please forgive my sin once more and appeal to the LORD your God, that He may remove this death from me.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: So Moses left Pharaoh’s presence and appealed to the LORD.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: And the LORD changed the wind to a very strong west wind that carried off the locusts and blew them into the Red Sea. Not a single locust remained anywhere in Egypt.
segments.speaker: The magicians

segments.text: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he would not let the Israelites go.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then the LORD said to Moses,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Stretch out your hand toward heaven, so that darkness may spread over the land of Egypt—a palpable darkness.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So Moses stretched out his hand toward heaven, and total darkness covered all the land of Egypt for three days.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: No one could see anyone else, and for three days no one left his place. Yet all the Israelites had light in their dwellings.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and said,
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Go, worship the LORD. Even your little ones may go with you; only your flocks and herds must stay behind.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh summoned Moses and

segments.text: But Moses replied,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: You must also provide us with sacrifices and burnt offerings to present to the LORD our God.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Even our livestock must go with us; not a hoof will be left behind, for we will need some of them to worship the LORD our God, and we will not know how we are to worship the LORD until we arrive.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he was unwilling to let them go.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Depart from me!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Pharaoh said to Moses.
Make sure you never see my face again, for on the day you see my face, you will die.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: As you say,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Moses replied,
I will never see your face again.
segments.speaker: Moses
